summary: 7 items surfaced from 23 qualifying stories; 2 story pages were blocked by anti-bot.

failed_stories.title: Show HN: I wrote a flight simulator in my own programming language
What causes lightning? The answer keeps getting more interesting

items.name: Bun
items.source_story_title: Bun's experimental Rust rewrite hits 99.8% test compatibility on Linux x64 glibc
items.summary: Bun runtime — experimental Rust rewrite reached 99.8% test compatibility on Linux x64 glibc.
items.why_included: Commenters were impressed that a huge runtime rewrite got this far this fast.
items.evidence: HN comments called the result “remarkable” and highlighted the 99.8% test-suite compatibility after starting from thousands of compiler errors.

items.name: Zed Editor Theme-Builder
items.summary: Zed theme builder — visual editor for high-contrast themes and fine-grained styling.
items.why_included: Commenters liked the inspector-driven workflow and the ability to make Zed genuinely high-contrast.
items.evidence: The thread praised the inspector as a game changer and said the theme builder finally made good high-contrast themes practical.

items.name: GrapheneOS
items.source_story_title: GrapheneOS fixes Android VPN leak Google refused to patch
items.summary: GrapheneOS — Android security OS that patched a VPN leak Google refused to fix.
items.why_included: The thread treated the fix as a real security win and discussed GrapheneOS as practical privacy infrastructure.
items.evidence: Commenters praised GrapheneOS’s release 2026050400 fix and repeatedly recommended supported Pixels for people who want a hardened Android stack.

items.name: Pixel 8
items.source_story_title: GrapheneOS fixes Android VPN leak Google refused to patch
items.summary: Pixel 8 phone — recommended GrapheneOS hardware pick thanks to MTE support.
items.why_included: The thread gave unusually concrete hardware advice for anyone buying a GrapheneOS-capable phone.
items.evidence: A commenter said Pixel 8 is preferable because it adds MTE support, while others warned that carrier-sold Pixels often lack unlockable bootloaders.

items.name: Ben Eater's 8-bit computer
items.summary: Ben Eater's 8-bit computer — hands-on DIY CPU project for learning computer architecture.
items.why_included: The TD4 thread singled it out as one of the best beginner resources for understanding CPUs.
items.evidence: A commenter called it “the most excellent ben-eater’s 8bit-computer” and linked it as a strong learning resource.

items.name: Nand to Tetris
items.summary: Nand to Tetris — from-first-principles systems course that builds a computer from logic gates.
items.why_included: It was recommended alongside Ben Eater’s project as a strong route into computer fundamentals.
items.evidence: Commenters grouped it with Ben Eater’s build as a top-tier path from simple gates to a working computer.

items.name: Why It Was Almost Impossible to Make the Blue LED
items.summary: YouTube explainer — the long road to making the blue LED.
items.why_included: A commenter explicitly recommended it as a good companion watch to the LEDs article.
items.evidence: The LED manufacturing thread described the video as “a non-frantic (related) video worth the watch.”
